title: Finite Z2 plaquette spectral-gap candidate search
claim_boundary: Finite Z2 lattice gauge toy benchmark only; no continuum Yang-Mills mass-gap claim.
question: Can a deterministic search over finite Z2 plaquette families identify gap profiles that remain stable across small finite-size and transverse-field perturbation checks?
success_criteria: Generate ranked finite candidates with reproducible exact diagonalization records.
Preserve Pauli dense replica agreement within numerical tolerance for every ranked record.
Write JSON, JSONL, CSV, Markdown summary, and per-candidate dossiers.
Keep every output clearly bounded to finite-system exploration.
kill_criteria: Pauli dense replica does not match direct dense Hamiltonian within tolerance.
Exact diagonalization residuals are not small enough for a finite benchmark.
Candidate rankings depend on missing or non-deterministic metadata.
Any generated dossier implies a continuum Yang-Mills proof or Clay-prize claim.
next_milestone: gaugegap-0004 noisy quantum simulator and hardware-readiness pipeline
